title: T-069 — Docs: TEC-092 ECO Phase-2 marker (Phase 1 out-of-scope)
prompt:
# T-069 — Docs: TEC-092 ECO Phase-2 marker (Phase 1 out-of-scope)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §4.4, §7A TEC-092
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Phase-2 marker note for ECO so prototypes are not orphaned.

Note links the three ECO prototypes (eco_screen, eco_change_request_modal, eco_approval_modal) and the §4.4 exclusion. Include suggested Phase-2 acceptance criteria scaffolding for future re-decomposition.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- design/specs/TEC-092-eco-phase2.md [create]

## Out of scope
- Implementation
- Prototype changes

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the note exists, when reviewed, then it confirms ECO is Phase-2 and lists the three prototypes.
2. Given the note, when planning Phase-2, then it provides scaffolding for re-decomposition.

## Test strategy
- Manual review.
- No automated tests.

## Risk red lines
- Do not draft Phase-1 ECO tasks.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
